topic.name: Height and Distance
topic.description: Trigonometry-based problems on angles of elevation and depression, shadows, ladders, and towers. Estimated reading 24 min; practice 40 min.

theory.introduction: Height and Distance problems apply basic trigonometry to find unknown heights or horizontal distances using angles of elevation (looking up) or depression (looking down). A right triangle is formed by the vertical height, horizontal distance, and the line of sight. Standard angles 30°, 45°, and 60° appear frequently, so knowing their sine, cosine, and tangent values speeds up solutions.
theory.explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

When an observer at point O looks at the top T of a tower, the angle between the horizontal and line of sight OT is the angle of elevation. If the observer is above the object, the angle between the horizontal and the downward line of sight is the angle of depression; it equals the angle of elevation from the object to the observer.

In the right triangle: tan θ = Height / Horizontal distance; sin θ = Height / Slant distance (hypotenuse); cos θ = Base / Hypotenuse.

Shadow problems treat the sun's rays as making angle θ with the ground, so Height / Shadow length = tan θ.

When an observer walks toward a tower and the angle of elevation changes from θ1 to θ2, the height can be found using h = d / (cot θ1 − cot θ2), where d is the distance walked toward the tower.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Surveying, architecture, aviation glide paths, navigation, and estimating building or tree heights without direct measurement use these ideas.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Using sin instead of tan for height when horizontal distance is given.
2) Confusing angle of depression with its complement.
3) Forgetting that depression from the top equals elevation from the bottom.
4) Mixing slant height with vertical height.
5) Not drawing a diagram before assigning opposite and adjacent sides.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• tan 30° = 1/√3, tan 45° = 1, tan 60° = √3.
• sin 30° = 1/2, sin 60° = √3/2, cos 30° = √3/2, cos 60° = 1/2.
• Angle of depression from top = angle of elevation from foot of object to observer.
• Ladder problems: vertical height = ladder length × sin(angle with ground).
• Always identify the right triangle and label opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse clearly.

formulas.title: Shadow relation
formulas.content: Height / Shadow length = tan(angle of elevation of sun)

formulas.title: Angle of depression
formulas.content: Angle of depression from top = angle of elevation from bottom to observer

formulas.title: Two-position formula
formulas.content: h = d / (cot θ1 − cot θ2) when observer moves distance d toward object and elevation changes from θ1 to θ2

shortcutTips.title: 45° shortcut
shortcutTips.tip: At 45° elevation, height equals horizontal distance because tan 45° = 1.

shortcutTips.title: 30–60 pair
shortcutTips.tip: Memorize: opposite 30° is half the hypotenuse; opposite 60° is (√3/2) times hypotenuse.

shortcutTips.title: Draw first
shortcutTips.tip: Sketch the right triangle and mark θ before choosing sin, cos, or tan.

shortcutTips.title: Depression equals elevation
shortcutTips.tip: Replace depression at the top with equal elevation at the lower point.

shortcutTips.title: Shadow tip
shortcutTips.tip: Tree height equals shadow length when the sun's elevation is 45°.

shortcutTips.title: Moving observer
shortcutTips.tip: When walking toward a tower, subtract cotangents: h = d/(cot θ1 − cot θ2).

solvedExamples.problem: From a point 20 m away, the angle of elevation of the top of a tower is 60°. Find the height of the tower.
solvedExamples.solution: The height of the tower is 20√3 m (approximately 34.64 m).

solvedExamples.problem: A ladder 10 m long leans against a wall making 60° with the ground. Find the height reached on the wall.
solvedExamples.solution: The ladder reaches a height of 5√3 m on the wall.

solvedExamples.problem: The angle of elevation of the sun is 45°. A pole casts a shadow 12 m long. Find the height of the pole.
solvedExamples.solution: The height of the pole is 12 m.

solvedExamples.problem: From the top of a 50 m cliff, the angle of depression of a boat is 30°. How far is the boat from the base of the cliff?
solvedExamples.solution: The boat is 50√3 m from the base of the cliff.
steps: Depression 30° ⇒ elevation from boat to cliff top is 30°.

exercise.title: General Questions
exercise.description: MCQ practice on angles of elevation and depression, shadows, ladders, and two-position observations.

questions.question: From a point 15 m from the base of a tower, the angle of elevation of the top is 45°. Find the height of the tower.
A ladder 12 m long rests against a wall at 60° to the ground. What height does it reach?
At 30° sun elevation, a tree casts a shadow 10√3 m. Find the tree height.
From 40 m away, the angle of elevation of a building top is 30°. Height of the building is:
A pole 8 m high casts a shadow 8 m long. The angle of elevation of the sun is:
From the top of a 30 m tower, the angle of depression of a car is 60°. How far is the car from the tower base?
A man 1.7 m tall observes a tower at 30° elevation. He stands 50 m from the base. Approximate tower height (ignore observer height)?
A kite is flying with 100 m of string at 30° elevation (string treated as straight). Height of the kite is:
If the angle of elevation of a cloud from a point 200 m above a lake is 30° and its reflection in the lake has depression 60°, the height of the cloud above the lake is:
questions.explanation: Let cloud height above lake = H. Using standard reflection setup: H = 200 + 200 = 400 m (observer 200 m above lake; angles 30° and 60° give H = 400 m).

questions.question: Walking 20 m toward a tower, the angle of elevation changes from 30° to 60°. Height of the tower is:
A tower subtends 60° at a point and 30° after moving 40 m toward it. Height of the tower is:
From two points 40 m apart on a straight line through the base, angles of elevation are 30° and 60° on the same side. Height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: Standard result: h = (distance × tan 30° × tan 60°)/(tan 60° − tan 30°) = 40×(1/√3)×√3/(√3 − 1/√3) = 20√3 m.

questions.question: A 20 m ladder reaches 10 m up a wall. The angle with the ground is:
Horizontal distance to a hilltop at 45° elevation is 25 m. Slant distance to the top is:
From a ship, the top of a lighthouse 60 m high has an angle of elevation of 30°. The ship is how far from the lighthouse base?
Two buildings 40 m and 60 m high. From the top of the shorter, the angle of elevation of the taller top is:
questions.explanation: Vertical difference = 20 m, horizontal separation assumed equal bases → if aligned, tan θ = 20/40 = 1/2.

questions.question: A man 2 m tall casts a shadow 2√3 m long. The sun's elevation angle is:
Walking 20 m toward a tower, the angle of elevation changes from 30° to 45°. The height of the tower is:
A tower stands on a cliff 25 m high. From a boat, the cliff top has elevation 30° and tower top 60°. Height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: Let horizontal distance = x. Cliff: tan 30° = 25/x. Tower top: tan 60° = (25+h)/x. Dividing: √3 = (25+h)/25 ⇒ h = 50/3 m.

questions.question: The angle of depression of the base of a 50 m tower from its top is:
questions.explanation: Depression to the base depends on horizontal distance; it is not fixed by height alone.

questions.question: From 100 m away, elevation to top is 45°. Slant distance to top is:
questions.explanation: Height = 100 m. Slant = 100√2 m by 45° triangle or hyp = 100/cos 45° = 100√2 m.

questions.question: A flagstaff 6 m high casts a shadow 2√3 m. A nearby building casts a shadow 10√3 m at the same time. Height of the building is:
questions.explanation: Sun angle same: 6/(2√3) = h/(10√3) ⇒ h = 30 m.

questions.question: An observer 1.5 m tall stands 25 m from a tower. The angle of elevation to the top is 30°. Total height of the tower is approximately:
questions.explanation: Height above eye level = 25 × tan 30° = 25/√3 ≈ 14.43 m. Total ≈ 14.43 + 1.5 ≈ 16 m.

questions.question: A rope tied to the top of a pole 12 m high makes 60° with the ground on taut ground support. Length of the rope is:
